title: Escape periods in path
body:
Unescaped periods in things like paths in tables can trigger failed man page builds.

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note

string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *baude* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [baude] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

> can trigger failed man page builds.

Shouldn't CI fail then? Do we not have a proper test for that, I thought that should be covered.
I think it was a golangci-lint version issue and we would have seen this upstream after an update.

It's not go lint because this is documentation.  Looking through things here, I could not root cause this.  I thought about adding a test looking for `.` inside paths, but it also had to be inside a table.  With no drop dead sure root cause, I just fixed the escapes like had been done elsewhere and moved on.

> It's not go lint because this is documentation. Looking through things here, I could not root cause this. I thought about adding a test looking for `.` inside paths, but it also had to be inside a table. With no drop dead sure root cause, I just fixed the escapes like had been done elsewhere and moved on.

Are you talking about this error?

```
FATAL: docs/source/markdown/podman-systemd.unit.5.md has a too-long table column; use 'man -l docs/build/man/podman-systemd.unit.5' and look for empty table cells.
```

It would be nice to include the failure message in the commit to know what was fixed. That error is being generated by ourselves as check in the Makefile, https://github.com/containers/podman/commit/11ffea313b659a12c0ee4d146afae264da3f2629

If that is not failing in CI this seems extremely concerning.
